key_points: Threat: EXTREME. Lebanon is now an active combat zone with Israeli ground forces operating in southern Lebanon and ongoing airstrikes across the country.
Trend: RAPIDLY ESCALATING. Military operations continue expanding geographically.
Air Travel: BEY: OPEN with partial operations. Middle East Airlines remains the primary carrier operating limited flights.
Ground Travel: Border crossings into Syria remain open but extremely congested, with long delays and significant refugee movement.

situation_assessment.assessment: EXTREME. Lebanon is now an active combat zone with Israeli ground forces operating in southern Lebanon and ongoing airstrikes across the country.
RAPIDLY ESCALATING. Military operations continue expanding geographically.
Hezbollah participation in the war, Israeli military operations, and the collapse of the previous ceasefire agreement.
Israeli airstrikes continued across Beirut suburbs and southern Lebanon, with additional displacement of civilians.
Continued airstrikes and ground operations expected. Infrastructure and transportation nodes remain vulnerable to targeting.
Israeli operations could expand further north as Israel attempts to degrade Hezbollah’s operational capability while Iranian forces remain engaged elsewhere.
BEY: OPEN with partial operations. Middle East Airlines remains the primary carrier operating limited flights.
Border crossings into Syria remain open but extremely congested, with long delays and significant refugee movement.

airports.name: Beirut Rafic Hariri International
airports.notes: OPEN with partial operations; MEA remains primary limited carrier.

borders.name: Overland routes to Syria
borders.notes: Open but extremely congested; significant displacement/traffic.

domestic_unrest.notes: Active conflict zone; displacement and security environment highly volatile.

energy_infra.notes: Infrastructure and transportation nodes vulnerable; outages possible.
